title: Integrate Camera Recording Directly into Live Short Upload Flow

requirements.summary: Add seamless 'Record & Upload Short' flow on UploadVideoPage that opens CameraRecordingModal, automatically marks as Short, populates form with recorded Blob, and allows immediate submission without leaving the page
acceptanceCriteria: The UploadVideoPage has a clearly labeled 'Record & Upload Short' button that opens the CameraRecordingModal.
After the user stops recording and confirms the clip in the modal, the recorded Blob is set as the selected video file in the upload form.
The 'Mark as Short' toggle is automatically enabled when a video is recorded via the camera recording flow.
The duration of the recorded clip is validated client-side; if it exceeds 60 seconds, a clear warning is shown and the Short toggle is unchecked.
The title and description fields are pre-populated or left editable so the user can complete and submit without re-selecting a file.
The entire flow — record, preview, confirm, fill form details, upload — is achievable without navigating away from the UploadVideoPage.
If the user cancels the modal, the upload form state is unchanged.

file_operations.description: Add a 'Record & Upload Short' button above or near the file upload input that opens the CameraRecordingModal. When the modal returns a recorded Blob: (1) set it as the selected video file in the upload form state, (2) automatically enable the 'Mark as Short' toggle, (3) extract the video duration from the Blob using a HTMLVideoElement, (4) validate the duration is ≤60s (show a warning toast and uncheck the Short toggle if >60s), (5) leave title and description fields editable so the user can complete the form and submit. If the user cancels the modal, do not modify the form state. Ensure the entire record-and-upload flow is continuous without navigation. Use the existing CameraRecordingModal component and useUploadVideo hook without modification.
